verdict_reasoning: ATG9A (autophagy-related protein 9A) is a multi-pass transmembrane protein that resides predominantly on the membranes of the autophagosome, late endosomes/lysosomes, trans-Golgi network, and recycling endosomes. It cycles between these endomembrane compartments to supply lipid for autophagosome biogenesis. No credible surface biotinylation, flow cytometry on intact non-permeabilized cells, or surface proteomics data supports stable plasma membrane residence. Checking all contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented stress/apoptosis-induced PM surface display; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no lineage-specific PM data; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — ATG9A is retrieved before lysosomal fusion; (4) dual_localization — cycling is among endomembrane compartments only, not to the PM outer leaflet; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no evidence of extracellular attachment. No therapeutic program targeting ATG9A on intact cell surfaces is known. Its multi-pass topology faces the cytoplasm/lumen of endomembrane vesicles, not the extracellular face.
